rationale: In the theory section, the citation is discussed explicitly and linked to a concrete empirical claim that higher raw-idea quality leads to much better market outcomes. The citing paper uses this result to ground its "objective promise of crowdsourcing" framing, which sets up its own contribution on incremental effects of labeling products as customer-ideated at the point of purchase. The cited study is thus serving as prior empirical grounding, not just a passing literature-list mention.
relation_description: The citing paper relies on Kornish and Ulrich (2014) as empirical evidence that raw idea quality materially drives downstream sales performance. This helps establish the baseline objective value of crowdsourcing before the citing paper examines an additional marketing-label effect.
